label: Go/NoGo
definition: A task in which subjects respond to Go cues and withhold responses to NoGo cues, commonly used to study response inhibition, action selection, and impulsivity.

label: Two-Alternative Forced Choice
definition: A task in which subjects choose between two alternatives based on sensory evidence, value, memory, or task rules.

label: Reversal Learning
definition: A task where reward contingencies change and subjects must update learned associations.

label: Delay Discounting
definition: A task measuring preference between smaller immediate rewards and larger delayed rewards.

label: Probability Discounting
definition: A task measuring choices between certain rewards and larger uncertain rewards.

label: Multi-Armed Bandit
definition: A task where subjects choose among options with uncertain reward distributions, often used to study exploration, exploitation, and value learning.

label: Set Shifting
definition: A task where subjects switch between rules, stimulus dimensions, or response mappings.

label: Stop-Signal Task
definition: A task that measures the ability to cancel an initiated action after a stop cue.

label: Stroop Task
definition: A conflict task where subjects respond to one stimulus dimension while suppressing an interfering dimension.

label: Flanker Task
definition: A conflict task where a central target is surrounded by congruent or incongruent flankers.

label: Classical Conditioning
definition: A task where a conditioned stimulus predicts an unconditioned stimulus, reward, aversive event, or neutral outcome.

label: Operant Conditioning
definition: A task where subjects learn actions based on consequences such as rewards or punishments.

label: Fear Conditioning
definition: A task where a neutral cue or context is associated with an aversive stimulus.

label: Extinction Learning
definition: A task where a previously learned cue-outcome association is weakened when the outcome is omitted.

label: Spatial Navigation
definition: A task involving movement through physical or virtual space to study spatial maps, memory, and navigation strategies.

label: Morris Water Maze
definition: A spatial learning task where rodents locate a hidden platform in a water maze.

label: Radial Arm Maze
definition: A maze task testing spatial working memory and reference memory using multiple arms.

label: T-Maze
definition: A maze task where subjects choose between left and right arms, often testing memory, value, or alternation.

label: Y-Maze
definition: A three-arm maze task often used to assess spontaneous alternation and spatial memory.

label: Object Recognition
definition: A task measuring recognition memory through exploration of familiar and novel objects.

label: Delayed Match-to-Sample
definition: A task where subjects remember a sample stimulus across a delay and select the matching stimulus.

label: Delayed Nonmatch-to-Sample
definition: A task where subjects remember a sample stimulus and later choose the nonmatching stimulus.

label: N-Back
definition: A working memory task where subjects detect whether the current stimulus matches one presented n trials earlier.

label: Reaching
definition: A task involving limb movement toward a target, often used to study motor planning and execution.

label: Center-Out Reaching
definition: A reaching task where movements originate from a central position and extend to one of several targets.

label: Grasping
definition: A task involving hand shaping, object interaction, grip force, or reach-to-grasp movements.

label: Locomotion
definition: A task or behavioral condition involving walking, running, treadmill movement, or free movement.

label: Skilled Reaching
definition: A task where animals learn and perform precise reaching movements, often for food pellets.

label: Joystick Task
definition: A task where subjects control a joystick to move a cursor or perform directional motor actions.

label: Speech Production
definition: A task involving overt speech, attempted speech, phoneme production, syllable production, or speech motor planning.

label: Visual Decision-Making
definition: A task where subjects make choices based on visual stimuli.

label: Visual Discrimination
definition: A task requiring discrimination between visual features such as orientation, contrast, motion, shape, or category.

label: Naturalistic Vision
definition: A task or passive-viewing condition using natural scenes, images, or videos.

label: Auditory Discrimination
definition: A task requiring discrimination of tones, sounds, phonemes, amplitude, frequency, or auditory categories.

label: Somatosensory Detection
definition: A task involving detection or discrimination of tactile, whisker, vibration, or touch stimuli.

label: Olfactory Discrimination
definition: A task requiring discrimination, detection, or categorization of odors.

label: Multisensory Integration
definition: A task involving integration or comparison of stimuli across sensory modalities.

label: Social Interaction
definition: A task or naturalistic condition involving interaction between animals or humans.

label: Social Preference
definition: A task measuring preference for social versus nonsocial stimuli or familiar versus novel conspecifics.

label: Open Field
definition: A freely moving exploration assay often used to measure locomotion, anxiety-like behavior, and exploratory patterns.

label: Elevated Plus Maze
definition: A maze assay with open and closed arms used to measure anxiety-like behavior.

label: Forced Swim
definition: A behavioral assay measuring swimming, climbing, and immobility in water.

label: Sucrose Preference
definition: A task measuring preference for sucrose solution over water, often used as an anhedonia-related assay.

label: Sleep/Wake State
definition: A recording condition or task involving sleep, wakefulness, REM, NREM, or arousal transitions.

label: Pupil/Arousal Tracking
definition: A task or condition where pupil size, running, whisking, or other arousal indicators are measured.

label: Resting State
definition: A condition where subjects are recorded without an explicit task, often used to study intrinsic dynamics and functional connectivity.

label: Oddball
definition: A task where rare target or deviant stimuli are embedded among frequent standard stimuli.

label: Motor Imagery
definition: A task where subjects imagine movements without overt execution, commonly used in BCI.

label: Seizure Monitoring
definition: A clinical recording condition involving seizure events, interictal activity, or epilepsy monitoring.

label: Language Comprehension
definition: A task involving listening to, reading, or understanding words, sentences, stories, or natural speech.

label: Emotion Recognition
definition: A task where subjects identify, rate, or respond to emotional stimuli.

label: Pain Task
definition: A task involving painful, thermal, mechanical, or nociceptive stimulation.

label: Value-Based Decision Making
definition: A task where subjects make choices based on subjective values, rewards, or utilities.

label: Closed-Loop Control
definition: A task with real-time neural feedback or brain-machine interface control.

label: Auditory Processing
definition: A task or passive listening condition involving auditory stimuli, sound processing, or tonotopy.

label: Temporal Difference Learning
definition: A task designed to study temporal difference prediction errors, value updating, and model-free reinforcement learning mechanisms.

label: Actor-Critic Task
definition: A task structure supporting actor-critic decomposition where separate systems compute value and policy.

label: Model-Based Planning
definition: A task requiring internal model construction and prospective evaluation of action sequences.

label: Foraging Task
definition: A task involving sequential patch exploitation and exploration decisions, often with depleting resources or travel costs.

label: Effort-Based Decision Making
definition: A task where subjects choose between options differing in required effort and reward magnitude.

label: Random Dot Motion
definition: A motion discrimination task using random dot kinematograms to study evidence accumulation and perceptual decision-making.

label: Detection Task
definition: A task requiring detection of weak or near-threshold sensory stimuli, often used to study signal detection and perceptual sensitivity.

label: Categorization
definition: A task requiring classification of stimuli into learned categories based on perceptual or abstract features.

label: Motor Sequence Learning
definition: A task involving learning and execution of sequential motor actions, often used to study procedural memory and skill acquisition.

label: Force Tracking
definition: A task requiring precise control and modulation of force output, often isometric, to study motor control and feedback.

label: Bimanual Coordination
definition: A task requiring coordinated movements of both hands or limbs, often to study interhemispheric coordination.

label: Oculomotor Task
definition: A task involving saccades, smooth pursuit, fixation, or other eye movements to study oculomotor control and visual attention.

label: P300 BCI
definition: A brain-computer interface paradigm using the P300 event-related potential for communication or control.

label: SSVEP BCI
definition: A brain-computer interface using steady-state visual evoked potentials for frequency-tagged visual selection.

label: Speech BCI
definition: A brain-computer interface for decoding speech, attempted speech, or speech imagery for communication.

label: Handwriting BCI
definition: A brain-computer interface decoding imagined or attempted handwriting movements for text generation.

label: Speech Perception
definition: A task involving listening to and processing speech sounds, words, or sentences.

label: Picture Naming
definition: A task where subjects name visually presented objects or pictures, used to study word production and lexical access.

label: Reading
definition: A task involving reading text, words, or sentences either silently or aloud to study language processing.

label: Sleep Staging
definition: A recording paradigm for classifying sleep stages including wake, N1, N2, N3 (NREM), and REM sleep.

label: Interictal Monitoring
definition: Clinical monitoring of epilepsy patients during seizure-free periods to identify interictal epileptiform discharges and seizure onset zones.

label: Intraoperative Mapping
definition: Neurophysiological recording during surgery for functional mapping of motor, sensory, or language areas.

label: Social Reward
definition: A task involving social rewards such as social approval, cooperation, or social feedback.

label: Face Processing
definition: A task involving perception, recognition, or emotional processing of faces.

label: Theory of Mind
definition: A task requiring inference about others' mental states, beliefs, intentions, or knowledge.

label: Free Behavior
definition: Recording during unconstrained, self-paced naturalistic behavior without explicit task structure.

label: Prey Capture
definition: A naturalistic hunting task where animals capture live or virtual prey, used to study appetitive behavior and visuomotor coordination.

label: Maternal Behavior
definition: Recording during maternal care behaviors such as pup retrieval, nursing, or nest building.

label: Receptive Field Mapping
definition: Systematic presentation of visual or auditory stimuli to characterize neuronal receptive field properties.

label: Natural Movie Viewing
definition: Passive viewing of naturalistic movies or video clips to study visual processing under natural conditions.

label: Tonotopic Mapping
definition: Systematic presentation of tones or sounds to characterize frequency tuning and tonotopic organization.

label: Mismatch Negativity
definition: An auditory paradigm where rare deviant stimuli violate established patterns, generating prediction error responses.

label: Neuropixels
definition: High-density silicon probe for extracellular electrophysiology recording

label: Calcium Imaging
definition: Optical imaging of neural activity using calcium indicators

label: Extracellular Electrophysiology
definition: Recording electrical activity outside neurons

label: EEG
definition: Electroencephalography recording brain electrical activity from scalp

label: ECoG
definition: Electrocorticography recording from cortical surface

label: fMRI
definition: Functional magnetic resonance imaging measuring blood oxygenation

label: Behavior Video
definition: Video recording of animal behavior

label: Pose Tracking
definition: Tracking body position and kinematics from video

label: Fiber Photometry
definition: Optical recording through fiber optic implant

label: iEEG
definition: Intracranial EEG with depth electrodes

label: Peri-Stimulus Time Histogram
definition: Compute firing rates aligned to behavioral events or stimuli, revealing temporal dynamics of neural responses.

label: Population Decoding
definition: Use machine learning classifiers (SVM, logistic regression, neural networks) to decode behavioral variables from population neural activity.

label: Dimensionality Reduction
definition: Project high-dimensional neural activity into lower-dimensional spaces (PCA, GPFA, LFADS) to reveal population dynamics and latent variables.

label: Generalized Linear Model
definition: Model neural firing as a function of task variables, stimuli, and history using generalized linear models with Poisson or negative binomial likelihoods.

label: Spectral Analysis
definition: Compute power spectra, coherence, and phase relationships in local field potentials or EEG/ECoG signals across frequency bands.

label: Spike-Field Coupling
definition: Analyze relationships between spike timing and LFP phase/amplitude, including spike-field coherence and phase-locking statistics.

label: Reinforcement Learning Model
definition: Fit RL models (Q-learning, actor-critic) to behavioral choice data to estimate learning rates, value estimates, and prediction errors.

label: Psychometric Analysis
definition: Fit psychometric curves to choice behavior as a function of stimulus strength or evidence to estimate perceptual sensitivity and bias.

label: Drift-Diffusion Model
definition: Fit evidence accumulation models to choice and reaction time data to estimate drift rate, boundary separation, and non-decision time.

label: Seizure Detection
definition: Detect epileptic seizures or interictal discharges from continuous EEG/ECoG/SEEG recordings using spectral or machine learning methods.

label: Speech Decoding
definition: Decode speech-related signals (phonemes, words, sentences) from neural activity recorded during speech production or perception.

label: Motor Imagery Classification
definition: Classify imagined movements from EEG/ECoG signals for brain-computer interface applications, using CSP, LDA, or deep learning methods.

label: Prefrontal Cortex
definition: Brain region: Prefrontal Cortex

label: Hippocampus
definition: Brain region: Hippocampus

label: Striatum
definition: Brain region: Striatum

label: Visual Cortex
definition: Brain region: Visual Cortex

label: Motor Cortex
definition: Brain region: Motor Cortex

label: Amygdala
definition: Brain region: Amygdala

label: Thalamus
definition: Brain region: Thalamus

label: Cerebellum
definition: Brain region: Cerebellum

label: Basal Ganglia
definition: Brain region: Basal Ganglia

label: Orbitofrontal Cortex
definition: Brain region: Orbitofrontal Cortex
